operating_state: Total revenue increased 21% to $45.3 billion driven by comparable sales growth of 21% and 22 net new warehouses opened since prior year. E-commerce comparable sales surged 41% while membership fees grew 11% to $901 million from new signups and Executive membership upgrades. Member renewal rates remained robust at 91% in the U.S. and Canada and 88% worldwide with broad-based strength across merchandise categories.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage declined 35 basis points to 11.18% primarily due to sales mix shift toward lower-margin ancillary businesses, partially offset by elimination of COVID-related incremental wages that ended February 28, 2021. SG&A expenses as percentage of net sales improved 107 basis points to 9.44% through sales leverage and reduced COVID wage costs. Operating income increased 41% reflecting strong sales growth despite permanent wage increases for warehouse employees effective March 1, 2021.
material_events: Board declared quarterly dividend increase to $0.79 per share paid May 14, 2021. Company repurchased 1.04 million shares for approximately $368 million during first thirty-six weeks and acquired distribution facility for $345 million to support logistics activities. Ended COVID-related incremental wage payments totaling $825 million since March 2020 with final purchase price allocation completed for March 2020 Innovel acquisition.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency movements positively impacted net sales by $1.0 billion with foreign-currency transaction gains totaling $6 million. Gasoline price increases contributed $984 million to sales growth with higher average price per gallon. Supply chain and logistics constraints affecting certain merchandise categories expected to continue while interest income decreased due to lower interest rates despite higher average cash balances.
forward_outlook: Management expects COVID-related supply and logistics constraints to adversely affect some merchandise categories for the foreseeable future. Capital expenditures expected between $3.3 billion and $3.5 billion during fiscal 2021, increased from prior guidance due to distribution facility acquisition. Company plans seven additional warehouse openings in remainder of fiscal 2021 with higher square footage growth rates internationally.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple employment-related class actions pending in California courts regarding wage and hour practices with immaterial accruals recorded. Opioid litigation consolidated in federal proceedings with various claims while securities litigation regarding internal controls disclosures dismissed with appeal pending. Construction projects have experienced delays due to COVID-19 impacts.
